verses.text: Paul, an apostle, writes to the church at Corinth with grace and peace. The greeting. He addresses them as God's people still.
Paul, an apostle, writes to the church at Corinth with grace and peace. The greeting. He addresses them as God's people still.
Blessed be 'the God of all comfort; who comforteth us... that we may be able to comfort' others. Comfort to give. God consoles us that we may console others.
Blessed be 'the God of all comfort; who comforteth us... that we may be able to comfort' others. Comfort to give. God consoles us that we may console others.
As Christ's sufferings abound, so does our consolation; our hope of you is stedfast. Shared comfort. Affliction and comfort flow together in Christ.
As Christ's sufferings abound, so does our consolation; our hope of you is stedfast. Shared comfort. Affliction and comfort flow together in Christ.
As Christ's sufferings abound, so does our consolation; our hope of you is stedfast. Shared comfort. Affliction and comfort flow together in Christ.
We were pressed beyond measure, that we should 'trust... in God which raiseth the dead.' Driven to God. Extremity teaches us to lean on Him alone.
We were pressed beyond measure, that we should 'trust... in God which raiseth the dead.' Driven to God. Extremity teaches us to lean on Him alone.
Who delivered us, and will deliver, ye 'helping together by prayer.' Saved by prayer. The saints' prayers share in the deliverance.
Who delivered us, and will deliver, ye 'helping together by prayer.' Saved by prayer. The saints' prayers share in the deliverance.
Our rejoicing is the testimony of conscience, that we walked 'in simplicity and godly sincerity.' Clear conscience. A sincere life is its own glad witness.
Our rejoicing is the testimony of conscience, that we walked 'in simplicity and godly sincerity.' Clear conscience. A sincere life is its own glad witness.
Our rejoicing is the testimony of conscience, that we walked 'in simplicity and godly sincerity.' Clear conscience. A sincere life is its own glad witness.
I purposed to come; was I fickle, that my yea should be yea and nay? No double-dealing. He defends the constancy of his word.
I purposed to come; was I fickle, that my yea should be yea and nay? No double-dealing. He defends the constancy of his word.
I purposed to come; was I fickle, that my yea should be yea and nay? No double-dealing. He defends the constancy of his word.
Our word was not yea and nay; 'all the promises of God in him are yea.' Sure promises. In Christ every promise of God stands firm.
Our word was not yea and nay; 'all the promises of God in him are yea.' Sure promises. In Christ every promise of God stands firm.
Our word was not yea and nay; 'all the promises of God in him are yea.' Sure promises. In Christ every promise of God stands firm.
God stablishes us, having 'sealed us, and given the earnest of the Spirit.' Sealed and kept. The Spirit is the pledge of our inheritance.
God stablishes us, having 'sealed us, and given the earnest of the Spirit.' Sealed and kept. The Spirit is the pledge of our inheritance.
To spare you I came not yet; 'we are helpers of your joy.' Gentle care. He delays not to lord it over them but to spare them.
To spare you I came not yet; 'we are helpers of your joy.' Gentle care. He delays not to lord it over them but to spare them.
